title: Implement functional customer management page with add/edit form

requirements.summary: Replace the placeholder CustomersPage with a functional customer management interface that displays a list of existing customers and provides an 'Add Customer' button to open a form dialog
acceptanceCriteria: CustomersPage displays a grid or table of existing customers
An 'Add Customer' button is visible and opens a form dialog
The page follows the same layout pattern as InventoryPage and MenuPage

file_operations.description: Replace placeholder content with a functional customer management page structure. Add page header with 'Customer Management' title and 'Add Customer' button. Include CustomerList component to display existing customers. Add Dialog component to show CustomerForm when adding or editing customers. Follow the same layout pattern as InventoryPage with stat cards showing total customers and active customers count. Use the same green color scheme and styling conventions.

requirements.summary: Create a CustomerForm component with fields for name, mobile number, email, reference source, preferences, and address, using react-hook-form for validation and state management
acceptanceCriteria: Form includes all required fields: name, mobile number, email, reference source, preferences, address
Form validates mobile number format and email format
Form uses the same styling and component patterns as IngredientForm and RecipeForm
Form submits customer data to backend via mutation

file_operations.description: Create a form component for adding and editing customers using react-hook-form. Include form fields for name (Input), mobile number (Input with phone validation pattern), email (Input with email type), reference source (Select or Input), preferences (Textarea), and address (Textarea). Map form data to CustomerInput type where contactInfo contains mobile and email, and preferences are included in the address or contactInfo field as per backend interface. Use useAddCustomer and useUpdateCustomer mutations from useQueries. Include form validation for required fields, mobile number format (10 digits), and email format. Add submit and cancel buttons. Follow the same styling and structure as IngredientForm and RecipeForm with Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent components.

requirements.summary: Create a CustomerList component that displays customer cards in a grid layout showing name, mobile, email, and action buttons for editing
acceptanceCriteria: CustomerList fetches and displays all customers using React Query
Each customer card shows name, mobile, email prominently
Edit button opens the CustomerForm dialog with pre-filled data
Component follows the same pattern as IngredientList and RecipeList

file_operations.description: Create a component that fetches customers using useCustomers hook and displays them in a grid layout with responsive columns. Render each customer as a Card showing name as title, contactInfo (mobile and email) in the content area, and an Edit button. Extract mobile and email from contactInfo field for display. Add Badge to show active status. Include edit handler that opens CustomerForm dialog with selected customer data pre-filled. Follow the same grid layout pattern as IngredientList and RecipeList using CSS Grid with gap-4 and responsive column counts.

requirements.summary: Add React Query hooks for customer data fetching and mutations (useCustomers, useAddCustomer, useUpdateCustomer) in useQueries.ts
acceptanceCriteria: useCustomers hook fetches customer list from backend
useAddCustomer mutation creates a new customer and invalidates customer queries
useUpdateCustomer mutation updates existing customer and invalidates queries
Hooks follow the same pattern as existing ingredient and recipe hooks

file_operations.description: Add three React Query hooks for customer management. Create useCustomers hook using useQuery that calls actor.getCustomers() with queryKey ['customers']. Create useAddCustomer mutation hook using useMutation that calls actor.addCustomer(sessionId, customerData) and invalidates ['customers'] query on success. Create useUpdateCustomer mutation hook using useMutation that calls actor.updateCustomer(sessionId, customerId, updatedData) and invalidates ['customers'] query on success. Ensure all mutations require sessionId from AuthContext. Follow the same pattern as useIngredients, useAddIngredient, and useUpdateIngredient hooks with proper error handling and type safety using Customer and CustomerInput types from backend.d.ts.
